summary: Russia launched seven Iskander-M ballistic missiles and 189 attack drones across Ukraine, striking Kremenchuk, Odesa region, and Zaporizhzhia, causing casualties and infrastructure damage. Ukrainian forces reported downing three missiles and 174 drones. A prisoner exchange returned 160 Ukrainian captives.
key_points: Russia fired seven Iskander-M ballistic missiles and 189 attack drones, hitting Kremenchuk, Odesa region and Zaporizhzhia, with three missiles and 174 drones intercepted (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).
Six people were injured in a ballistic attack on Zaporizhzhia (Source: @UkraineNow/82213).
A drone strike damaged the Russian chemical plant Azot in Novomoskovsk and injured a woman (Source: @UkraineNow/82184).
Four groups of UAVs from Kherson moved toward Nikopol (Source: @eRadarrua/98287).
Russia and Ukraine exchanged 160 prisoners of war (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31187).
Ukrainian forces reported a ballistic missile over Kremenchuk and multiple Shahed attacks on the city and its dam (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68070, @mon1tor_ua/68065).
Ukrainian intelligence reported sabotage of Russian vessels Volga and Vyatka in occupied Crimea (Source: @UkraineNow/82191).

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched a coordinated strike of seven Iskander-M ballistic missiles and 189 attack drones, hitting Kremenchuk, Odesa region and Zaporizhzhia, causing civilian injuries and power outages. Ukrainian air defenses intercepted three missiles and 174 drones, limiting further damage. A prisoner exchange returned 160 Ukrainian captives to Kyiv.

points: Ukrainian air defenses shot down three Iskander-M missiles and 174 attack drones during the June 26 strike, preventing additional strikes on key infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).
Six civilians were injured in Zaporizhzhia after a ballistic missile attack on the city (Source: @UkraineNow/82213).
A drone attack on the Russian Azot chemical plant in Novomoskovsk damaged the facility, a transmission line and a residential house, injuring a woman (Source: @UkraineNow/82184).
Ukrainian forces reported a ballistic missile over Kremenchuk and multiple Shahed attacks on the city and its dam, causing a partial blackout (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68070, @mon1tor_ua/68065).
Four groups of UAVs from the Kherson direction moved toward Nikopol, indicating continued pressure on the southern front (Source: @eRadarrua/98287).
Russia deployed seven Iskander-M ballistic missiles and 189 attack drones across Ukraine on 26 June, targeting industrial and civilian sites (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).
Russian forces launched a ballistic missile at Mirgorod, as reported by local monitoring (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68077).
Russian UAVs were observed heading from the Black Sea toward Odesa and the Zatoka area (Source: @kpszsu/66508).
Russian naval assets Volga and Vyatka, intended for underwater surveillance and mine deployment, were sabotaged in occupied Crimea (Source: @UkraineNow/82191).
Russian artillery and drone strikes on fuel stations in Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk regions continued throughout June, with multiple incidents recorded (Source: @eRadarrua/98259).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported over Kharkiv region heading toward Kupiansk, indicating active aerial operations in the east (Source: @kpszsu/66513).
Four groups of UAVs operated near Izium, moving westward, suggesting pressure on the front line near the city (Source: @eRadarrua/98289).
A UAV from the Black Sea approached the Odesa region, targeting the Zatoka area (Source: @kpszsu/66508).
Ukrainian forces observed a UAV over Sumy near Ribtsi Kab heading toward Malokaterynivka (Source: @eRadarrua/98319).
A UAV group was seen over the border of Chernihiv and Sumy regions heading toward Poltava, indicating cross‑regional reconnaissance (Source: @kpszsu/66525).
UAVs were reported over northern Kyiv region near Krasatychi, moving toward Zhytomyr, showing activity north of the capital (Source: @kpszsu/66490).
A UAV from the Black Sea moved north over Kyiv region toward the Chernobyl zone, then southward (Source: @kpszsu/66489).
Ukrainian monitoring noted UAV activity over the Chernihiv‑Sumy border area, heading toward Poltava (Source: @kpszsu/66525).
A UAV was observed over the northern Kyiv area near the Chernobyl exclusion zone, indicating potential reconnaissance (Source: @kpszsu/66489).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV over the northern Kyiv region, near the Chernobyl area, heading south (Source: @kpszsu/66489).
Four UAV groups from Kherson moved toward Nikopol, increasing pressure on the southern front (Source: @eRadarrua/98287).
Ukrainian forces reported a ballistic missile strike on Kremenchuk's oil refinery, causing a partial blackout (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68056).
Multiple drone attacks targeted fuel stations in Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk regions throughout June, with at least one civilian fatality (Source: @eRadarrua/98259).
A Shahed drone hit the Kremenchuk dam, threatening water infrastructure (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68065).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over the Odesa region heading toward the Zatoka area (Source: @kpszsu/66508).
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted three Iskander-M missiles and 174 of 189 attack drones during the large‑scale strike on 26 June (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).
Russian UAVs were reported operating from the Black Sea toward Odesa and the Zatoka area, indicating maritime aerial threats (Source: @kpszsu/66508).
Ukrainian forces observed multiple UAV groups moving from the south and east toward key front‑line cities, suggesting coordinated aerial reconnaissance (Source: @kpszsu/66513, @eRadarrua/98289).
A UAV attack on the Russian Azot plant in Novomoskovsk demonstrated the reach of Ukrainian strike capabilities into Russian‑held territory (Source: @UkraineNow/82184).
Ukrainian monitoring recorded UAV activity over the northern Kyiv region, potentially probing air defense gaps (Source: @kpszsu/66489).

key_developments: Russia launched seven Iskander-M ballistic missiles and 189 attack drones across Ukraine, striking Kremenchuk, Odesa region and Zaporizhzhia, with three missiles and 174 drones intercepted (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).
Ukrainian air defenses downed three missiles and 174 drones, limiting further damage to civilian infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).
A prisoner exchange returned 160 Ukrainian captives, including senior officers, after months of detention (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31187).
Multiple ballistic missile and Shahed attacks targeted Kremenchuk, its dam and oil refinery, causing a partial blackout and civilian injuries (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68070, @mon1tor_ua/68065).

Ukrainian UAVs operated in the east, north and south, including groups near Izium, Kharkiv and Kherson, indicating sustained aerial pressure on multiple fronts (Source: @eRadarrua/98289, @kpszsu/66513, @eRadarrua/98287).
Russian naval assets Volga and Vyatka were sabotaged in occupied Crimea, reducing Russia's underwater surveillance capability (Source: @UkraineNow/82191).
Drone attacks on Russian Azot chemical plant in Novomoskovsk damaged the facility and injured a civilian, showing Ukrainian strike reach (Source: @UkraineNow/82184).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces continue to employ long‑range ballistic missiles and large UAV swarms, targeting critical infrastructure in central and southern Ukraine, while also attempting maritime and naval disruptions in Crimea. Ukrainian air defenses have shown resilience, intercepting the majority of aerial threats, yet repeated strikes on energy facilities and dams pose ongoing risks to civilian populations and logistics. The sustained UAV activity across multiple fronts suggests Russia is maintaining pressure and reconnaissance, but Ukrainian counter‑UAV operations indicate growing capability to contest airspace.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine has mobilized air defense assets to intercept the bulk of incoming missiles and drones, while local authorities report partial blackouts and civilian injuries, prompting emergency response measures. International partners have highlighted the prisoner exchange as a confidence‑building step, though broader diplomatic efforts remain focused on sustaining aid and reconstruction commitments. Russian sabotage of naval assets in Crimea may provoke heightened security measures in the Black Sea region.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring indicates a pattern of coordinated missile‑drone attacks timed with strategic infrastructure strikes, likely aimed at degrading Ukraine's energy and logistics networks. UAV trajectories from the Black Sea toward Odesa and from Kherson toward Nikopol suggest a focus on southern supply routes. The successful interception of most aerial threats underscores the effectiveness of Ukrainian radar and missile systems, but continued Russian missile launches highlight the need for reinforced early‑warning capabilities.
